old_claim.note: Textiles & Weaving provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No weaving dependency remains because the direct Dzudzuana evidence consists of individual spun, knotted, and dyed flax fibers rather than woven cloth.

source_shape.source_locator: Title and abstract record describe spun, dyed, and knotted wild-flax fibers from Upper Paleolithic cave layers.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The direct color evidence is microscopic fiber material and does not establish a woven textile structure.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Fiber coloring can precede cloth construction, and the find predates the graph's scoped preserved weaving by more than twenty millennia.

ontology_before: Fiber dyeing required a much later preserved woven-cloth tradition.
ontology_after: The Dzudzuana color evidence stands as an earlier fiber process without a weaving prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: natural_dyes no longer directly depends on textiles_weaving.
would_reject_if: The Dzudzuana colored fibers were shown to come exclusively from woven cloth produced after the Catalhoyuk evidence.
short_reason: The direct evidence is colored fiber, not woven cloth.
